evidences.title: How to Use Coinbase Advanced - to Buy, Sell, Limit Order, Market Order
evidences.content: can scroll up and down to zoom in or out of the graph by clicking this over here. The D that stands for duration. You can select each candlestick that you're seeing. Each one of these is a candlestick to a number of minutes or hours or day. If you select a shorter duration, you'll only see more recent price action. So, notice how I'm just looking at the last few days of action. You can tell by where my mouse is over here. Now, if I go to one day candlesticks, you'll see that you can see a larger duration all the way back, in this case to a couple of years ago. And by scrolling up and down with your mouse, you can choose to zoom in or out of the graph. Now, let's have a look at these confusing columns over here. evidences.title: Coinbase Advanced dashboard overview
evidences.content: ### Time range You can see the pricing history and trading volume of an asset over a specific span of time. | The candles on the y-axis (vertical line) reflect the high and low price of an asset for a specific time interval, which can be changed by selecting from the dropdown in the upper left-hand corner of the price chart | | --- | | The x-axis (the horizontal line) allows you to see the trading volume over a specific date range, which can be changed in the bottom left-hand corner of the chart | | ### Chart types The candlestick chart displays the high, low, open, and closing prices of an asset for a specific time frame. [...] O (open) is the opening price of the asset at the beginning of the specified period. H (high) is the highest trading price of the asset in that period. L (low) is the lowest trading price of the asset in that period. C (close) is the closing price of the asset at the end of the specific period. Check out this guide on how to read candlestick charts for more information. Rates are returned in grouped buckets. Refer to the Creating API Keys section of our Coinbase App Authentication docs for information on how to generate your Bearer Token. #### Path Parameters The trading pair (e.g. 'BTC-USD'). #### Query Parameters The UNIX timestamp indicating the start of the time interval. The UNIX timestamp indicating the end of the time interval. evidences.title: Coinbase Data in R & Candlestick Charts - GitHub Pages
evidences.content: Setting the granularity to 60 means price data every minute. Having the granularity at 300 would be every 5 minutes price data. For the candlestick plotting, I am doing daily data. The granularity is at 86400. ``` library(tidyr) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(plotly) library(jsonlite) library(glue) ``` [...] ## ## Creating A Function For Producing Candlestick Charts From Coinbase Data What if I do not want to write a .csv file and want to work with the data right away? I have modified the blog post script. It is a function which takes in a crypto trading pair as the only input. The function extracts crypto data from Coinbase’s API. Column names are renamed and UNIX times are converted into a datetime. Candlestick price charts are produced as the output with the use of plotly. Plotly is nice as it is interactive compared to ggplot2. About Granularity Reference: At first, I was confused about the granularity part. I asked myself why it was 86400. This 86400 represents 86400 seconds in a day. Setting the granularity to 86400 means that the obtained data would be daily price data. [...] ### Some Function Calls Not that the function for the plot is created. Some function cal...

evidences.title: Get aggregated candles data per instrument - Coinbase Developer Documentation
evidences.content: light logo dark logo Coinbase International Exchange API ##### REST API ##### FIX API ##### WEBSOCKET FEED cURL `curl --request GET \ --url `{ "aggregations": [ { "start": "2024-04-23T00:00:00Z", "open": "62884.4", "high": "64710.6", "low": "62884.4", "close": "63508.4", "volume": "3253.9983" } ] }` # Get aggregated candles data per instrument Retrieves a list of aggregated candles data for a given instrument, granularity and time range cURL [...] instrumentsService.getAggregatedCandles({ instrumentId: 'BTC-PERP', granularity: 'ONE_DAY', start: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', }).then(async (response) => { console.log('Aggregated Candles: ', response); })` #### Path Parameters Identifies the instrument by name (e.g., `BTC-PERP`) `BTC-PERP` #### Query Parameters The aggregation period of the candles data. End timestamp in ISO 8601 timestamp format (e.g. 2024-03-01T00:00:00Z). `ONE_DAY` `SIX_HOUR` `TWO_HOUR` `ONE_HOUR` `THIRTY_MINUTE` `FIFTEEN_MINUTE` `FIVE_MINUTE` `ONE_MINUTE` Start timestamp in ISO 8601 timestamp format (e.g. `2024-03-01T00:00:00Z`) `2024-03-01T00:00:00Z` End timestamp in ISO 8601 timestamp format (e.g. `2024-03-01T00:00:00Z`) `2024-03-01T00:00:00Z` #### Response Instrum...

evidences.title: Get index candles - Coinbase Developer Documentation
evidences.content: The pass phrase affiliated with the API Key A HMAC SHA-256 signature using the API Key secret on the string TIMESTAMP, METHOD, REQUEST\_PATH, BODY The timestamp of when the request is being made #### Path Parameters Identifies the index by name (e.g., `COIN50`) `COIN50` #### Query Parameters The aggregation period of the candles data `ONE_DAY` `ONE_HOUR` Start timestamp in ISO 8601 timestamp format (e.g. `2024-10-21T00:00:00Z`) `2024-10-21T00:00:00Z` End timestamp in ISO 8601 timestamp format (e.g. `2024-10-31T00:00:00Z`) `2024-10-31T00:00:00Z` #### Response Index Candle list Show child attributes Was this page helpful? light logo dark logo [...] cURL `curl --request GET \ --url \ --header 'CB-ACCESS-KEY: <api-key>' \ --header 'CB-ACCESS-PASSPHRASE: <api-key>' \ --header 'CB-ACCESS-SIGN: <api-key>' \ --header 'CB-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP: <api-key>'` `{ "aggregations": [ { "start": "2024-10-31T00:00:00Z", "open": "311.4512360891112", "high": "312.4418815371496", "low": "297.4296073392959", "close": "299.5950052685418" } ] }` `const indexService = new IndexService(client); indexService.getIndexCandles({ index: 'COIN50', granularity: 'ONE_DAY', start: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z', }).then(async...

evidences.title: Quickstart - Fathom API
evidences.content: Copy ``` curl \curl \ -H "X-Api-Key: YOUR_API_KEY" \ -H "X-Api-Key: YOUR_API_KEY" \ -d include_transcript=true \ -d include_transcript=true \ -d recorded_by[]=me@mydomain.com \ -d recorded_by[]=me@mydomain.com \ -d created_after=2024-08-01T00:00:00Z \ -d created_after=2024-08-01T00:00:00Z \ -d created_before=2024-09-01T00:00:00Z -d created_before=2024-09-01T00:00:00Z # include_transcript=true: get transcripts in the response# include_transcript=true: get transcripts in the response# recorded_by[]=me@mydomain.com: meetings you recorded# recorded_by[]=me@mydomain.com: meetings you recorded# created_after/before: August date range# created_after/before: August date range ``` [...] ``` [...] ```
